content:
# iter-322 → next-plan recommendations

## CRITICAL / must-fix (act THIS plan iter)

1. **FBC `restrictedCartesianAffinePushout` statement mismatch (lvb-fbc322 must-fix).**
   Lean proves the tautological scheme-level `IsPullback` (`(IsPullback.of_hasPullback _ _).flip`), NOT
   the affine ring `IsPushout` with corner `A_σ⊗_R R'` the blueprint asserts. The downstream heart
   `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` needs the ring/tensor content. **Decision required:** either
   (a) re-sign the Lean decl to the ring-pushout form (square + `commRingCat_isPushout_iff_mathlib` +
   affineness of V/S/S'; decl is NOT protected) and re-prove, or (b) re-state the blueprint block to the
   weaker geometric square + push the ring content into a separate lemma. A `% NOTE:` already flags this in
   the chapter. Do NOT send a prover at `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` until this is resolved — the
   heart consumes whichever form you pick.

2. **FBC `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma` signature missing `[Finite κ]` (auditor MAJOR).**
   Its documented 3-step proof calls `coverInterOpen_baseChange_eq` (which requires `[Finite κ]`), but the
   signature `{κ : Type}(σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀)` lacks it. A prover cannot close the body as-written. Add `[Finite κ]`
   (the Čech call site has it automatically) before re-dispatching. Refactor/prover edit, not review.

## Closest-to-completion targets

- **FBC `openImmersion_beckChevalley` (NEW leaf, L705)** — now the single gate for `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`.
  Genuine open-immersion Beck–Chevalley sheaf iso (factor `p'^* g'^* ≅ gV^* p^*` + open-immersion adjunction
  iso, vanishing off preimage). This is the real content — frontier candidate. After it closes (+`[Finite κ]`
  added), `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma` is a mechanical assembly (range transport via
  `Scheme.Hom.opensRange_pullbackFst` + CLOSED `coverInterOpen_baseChange_eq` + `IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq`).
- **PTC `cmp_leg` core C** — fully isolated, mostly-proven (22-line verified prefix). Residual = `leftAdjointUniq`
  composite-adjunction-unit mate calculus. This is the named escalation point.

## Promising approaches needing more work

- **PTC core C escalation (planner's pre-declared next step).** The blocker: plain sheafification unit `η` does
  not syntactically align with `adjB`'s `Adjunction.comp` composite-adjunction unit, so
  `unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app` / `leftAdjointUniq_hom_app_counit` don't fire under `simp only`. Next: expand via
  `Adjunction.comp_unit_app` FIRST to align the units, THEN the two `@[simp]` `leftAdjointUniq` lemmas. Needs
  interactive LSP — but LSP times out on this file. Consider the scratch-loop on a file importing the TOS olean
  with ONLY core C's goal (cmp_leg residual) standalone, so the goal is small enough for `lean_goal`/`lean_multi_attempt`.

## Do NOT retry without structural change

- **PTC homEquiv-transpose route (analogist iter-322 recipe) — MECHANICALLY DEAD.** `homEquiv_naturality_left`
  whnf-detonates (200k-hb) and doesn't match; the deprecated `Sheaf.val` coercion makes the transposed goal
  ill-typed at `instances` transparency, breaking every kabstract. The ≤40-LOC homEquiv shortcut transposes only
  the easy direction and cannot reach the `Fp_h.map(cmp_f)` form. Use the hand-reduction (cancel_mono → Sq4 →
  unit.naturality_assoc → PC cancel) — already landed — and escalate core C directly. NOT another homEquiv attempt.
- **PTC `pullbackTensorMap_restrict` assembly** — gated on `cmp_leg`. Do NOT attempt the slide-into-giant-goal
  until core C closes; the `Scheme.Modules.pullback ≟ SheafOfModules.pullback` whnf wall under `respectTransparency
  false` is re-confirmed (3rd+ iter). Fix is the continuation-lemma split with default transparency + `(C:=…)`
  re-pin on every `⊗ₘ` — but only after `cmp_leg` is done.

## Blueprint coverage debt (1-to-1) — `archon dag-query unmatched` (385 total; new this iter)

- `AlgebraicGeometry.coverOpen_baseChange_eq` (private, FBC) — per-member open-immersion base-change range identity.
  Uses: `Scheme.isPullback_of_openCover`, `IsPullback.of_bot`, `IsPullback.of_iso`, `IsPullback.isoPullback_inv_fst`,
  `IsOpenImmersion.image_preimage_eq_preimage_image_of_isPullback`, `Scheme.Hom.opensRange_pullbackFst`. The per-member
  content of `lem:coverinteropen_basechange_eq`.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.coe_iInf_of_finite` (private, FBC) — `↑(⨅) = ⋂` for `[Finite κ]`. Uses `isOpen_iInter_of_finite`.
  `\mathlibok`-adjacent; planner may inline.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.cmp_leg` (private, PTC) — per-leg composition coherence, transpose of
  `pullbackValIso_comp`. Needs `\label+\lean+\uses` block under `lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange`. Uses:
  `pullbackValIso_comp`, `Adjunction.unit.naturality_assoc`, `cancel_mono`, `Iso.hom_inv_id_app`; residual core C
  depends on `Adjunction.unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app` / `leftAdjointUniq_hom_app_counit`.
  → blueprint-writer dispatch (lvb-ptc322 major): add `cmp_leg` block + expand `lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange`
    sketch (per-leg M/N split, `δ_h`-natural / `tensorHom_comp_tensorHom`, core-C mate calculus).

## Blueprint prose fixes (writer, non-gating)

- `lem:coverinteropen_basechange_eq` prose: "every index family" → "every *finite* index family" (`[Finite κ]` is
  mathematically necessary — frame-of-opens infimum). lvb-fbc322 minor.

## Off-front / held (non-gating, do not open this iter)

- Blueprint-doctor broken crefs: `Picard_GlueDescent.tex` (2), `Picard_QuotScheme.tex` (12) — HELD QuotScheme/
  GlueDescent lanes. Repoint when those lanes open.
- FBC `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (leaf-1, abstract left-adjoint flat-exactness) — off reduce-to-absolute path.
- FlatBaseChange split-then-restore; genericFlatness prep; FBC general-S 02KH + SS lift.
- ~385 isolated `lean_aux` (held backlog).

## Convergence signal (for progress-critic next iter)
- **FBC**: ~18 iters vs 6–14 est, sorry net UP (5→6) again. BUT closed 2 carved blocks (gate met) + 2 helpers =
  real structural advance, not pure churn. The frontier is now honest: `openImmersion_beckChevalley` is the one
  genuine open leaf; the rest is assembly + the re-sign decision. Watch the must-fix #1 resolution.
- **PTC**: `cmp_leg`/`pullbackTensorMap_restrict` residual for 4 consecutive iters, sorry 1→2. The de-risk
  (homEquiv) did NOT land mechanically. Core C is now isolated and named — next iter MUST execute the core-C
  escalation (Adjunction.comp_unit_app alignment), not generate more diagnosis. If core C walls again on the
  unit-alignment, that is a STUCK signal → mathlib-analogist on the `leftAdjointUniq`/`Adjunction.comp`-unit
  alignment specifically, or user escalation.
